title: Australia Increases Penalties for Social Media Companies
subtitle: The government is taking stronger action as many young people continue to bypass age restrictions.

summary.en: Australia is doubling the fines for social media companies that fail to keep children under 16 off their platforms. The government argues that tech giants are not doing enough to follow the law. Despite the removal of millions of accounts, research shows that most teenagers are still finding ways to access these sites. New rules will give officials more power to demand information from companies. Other countries are now looking at Australia's approach as they consider their own safety laws.
summary.zh: 澳洲政府將針對未能阻止十六歲以下兒童使用其平台的社群媒體公司，將罰款金額提高一倍。政府認為科技巨頭在遵守法律方面做得不夠。儘管已有數百萬個帳號被移除，研究顯示大多數青少年仍能找到方法存取這些網站。新規定將賦予官員更大權力，要求企業提供相關資訊。其他國家目前正密切關注澳洲的做法，並研擬各自的相關安全法規。

paragraphs.en: The Australian government has announced a major increase in the penalties for social media companies that fail to stop children under 16 from using their platforms. The proposed changes will double the maximum fine for these companies to $99 million. This move comes as officials argue that big technology firms are not doing enough to protect young people from harmful content online.
paragraphs.zh: 澳洲政府宣布大幅提高社群媒體公司的罰則，針對未能阻止十六歲以下兒童使用其平台的業者進行懲處。這項擬議的變更將使這些公司的最高罰款金額翻倍，達到九千九百萬澳幣。此舉源於官員認為大型科技公司在保護年輕人免受網路有害內容侵害方面，做得還遠遠不夠。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, the government believes these higher fines will force companies to take the law more seriously. The Prime Minister, Anthony Albanese, stated that while he is happy to see a global shift in how countries think about online safety, the current efforts by tech companies are not enough. He noted that there are still too many children using these platforms, despite the rules that were introduced on December 10.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報 (The Guardian)》報導，澳洲政府認為提高罰款將迫使企業更嚴肅看待法律規範。總理艾班尼斯 (Anthony Albanese) 表示，雖然他樂見全球各國對網路安全觀念的轉變，但科技公司目前的努力仍顯不足。他指出，儘管十二月十日已實施相關規定，但仍有太多兒童在使用這些平台。

paragraphs.en: Since the ban began, more than 5 million accounts belonging to people under 16 have been removed, deactivated, or restricted. However, the government admits that these numbers do not tell the whole story. Research suggests that the majority of teenagers are still finding ways to bypass—or get around—the age restrictions. This has led the government to propose further reforms that will give the eSafety commissioner more power. Under these new rules, the commissioner will be able to demand documents and information from social media companies to prove they are working to stop children from opening accounts. They will also be able to get information from other businesses, such as app stores.
paragraphs.zh: 自禁令實施以來，已有超過五百萬個十六歲以下使用者的帳號被移除、停用或限制。然而，政府坦言這些數據並未呈現全貌。研究顯示，大多數青少年仍能找到繞過年齡限制的方法。這促使政府提出進一步改革，賦予電子安全專員 (eSafety commissioner) 更大的權力。根據新規定，專員將有權要求社群媒體公司提供文件與資訊，以證明其確實致力於阻止兒童註冊帳號。此外，專員也能向應用程式商店等其他企業調閱相關資料。

paragraphs.en: Despite these government efforts, a study from the University of Newcastle has raised questions about how well the ban is working. The research, which was published in the BMJ, looked at more than 400 teenagers between the ages of 12 and 17. The study found that more than 80% of children under 16 were still using social media three months after the law started. The authors of the study concluded that there was limited success in stopping young people from using these apps. Many teenagers reported that they could easily avoid the age checks. For example, only a very small number of young users were asked to provide a photo of an official ID card. Most were simply asked to state their age or upload a regular photo, which is very easy to fake.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管政府做出這些努力，紐卡索大學 (University of Newcastle) 的一項研究仍對禁令的成效提出質疑。這項發表於《英國醫學期刊 (BMJ)》的研究調查了四百多名十二至十七歲的青少年。研究發現，在法律實施三個月後，仍有超過八成的十六歲以下兒童在使用社群媒體。研究作者總結認為，在阻止年輕人使用這些應用程式方面，成效相當有限。許多青少年表示，他們可以輕易避開年齡查核。例如，僅有極少數的年輕使用者被要求提供官方身分證件照片，大多數人僅被要求自行填寫年齡或上傳普通照片，而這些方式極易造假。

paragraphs.en: Australia’s decision to be the first country to create such a law has attracted attention from around the world. Other nations are now watching closely to see how these rules develop. The United Kingdom has already announced plans for a similar ban for those under 16, which is expected to start in 2027. France has also passed legislation to limit social media access for children under 15, with rules that include parental permission. Other countries, including Spain, Poland, Denmark, Malaysia, and Slovenia, are also considering similar proposals to keep young people safe.
paragraphs.zh: 澳洲決定成為全球首個制定此類法律的國家，已引起國際關注。其他國家正密切觀察這些規則的發展。英國已宣布計畫於二○二七年起，針對十六歲以下青少年實施類似禁令。法國也已通過立法，限制十五歲以下兒童使用社群媒體，規定包括需取得家長同意。包括西班牙、波蘭、丹麥、馬來西亞與斯洛維尼亞在內的其他國家，也正在考慮採取類似提案，以維護年輕人的安全。

paragraphs.en: For the Australian government, the goal is to lead the world in online safety. The Prime Minister emphasized that the new, higher fines show how serious the country is about this issue. By bringing the penalties in line with other consumer laws, the government hopes to create a stronger system that companies cannot ignore. The eSafety commissioner is currently investigating several major platforms, including Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube, to see if they are following the current rules.
paragraphs.zh: 對澳洲政府而言，目標是引領全球網路安全。總理強調，提高罰款金額顯示了政府對此議題的重視。透過將罰則與其他消費者保護法規對齊，政府希望建立一套企業無法忽視的強大體系。電子安全專員目前正針對臉書 (Facebook)、Instagram、TikTok、Snapchat 與 YouTube 等數個大型平台進行調查，以確認其是否遵守現行規定。

paragraphs.en: As the debate continues, the focus remains on the balance between technology and child protection. While the government pushes for stricter enforcement, the evidence suggests that technology companies face a difficult challenge in verifying the ages of their users. Whether these new, higher fines will lead to better technology or just more complex ways for teenagers to get around the rules remains to be seen. For now, the Australian government is committed to its path, hoping that its world-leading approach will eventually create a safer digital environment for all young people.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著辯論持續進行，焦點仍集中在科技發展與兒童保護之間的平衡。儘管政府推動更嚴格的執法，但證據顯示，科技公司在驗證使用者年齡方面面臨艱鉅挑戰。這些更高額的罰款究竟會促使技術升級，還是僅會導致青少年發展出更複雜的規避手段，仍有待觀察。目前，澳洲政府仍堅定執行其政策，期望這種領先全球的做法，最終能為所有年輕人創造一個更安全的數位環境。

keyTerms.definitionEn: An action or step taken to achieve a specific goal.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（移動），這裡作名詞，指政府採取的政策行動。文中：This move comes as officials argue that big technology firms are not doing enough to protect young people from harmful content online.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To reveal or show the full truth or reality of a situation.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「告訴」，這裡指數據或現象背後所揭露的事實。文中：However, the government admits that these numbers do not tell the whole story.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Consistent with or matching something else.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個慣用語，表示將罰款標準調整到與其他法律規範一致的程度。文中：By bringing the penalties in line with other consumer laws, the government hopes to create a stronger system that companies cannot ignore.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Australia to double penalty for social media ban breaches to $99m as tech giants accused of ‘not doing enough’

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the primary reason the Australian government is increasing fines for social media companies?

options.A: To generate more tax revenue for the government.
options.B: To ensure companies take the legal requirements for child protection more seriously.
options.C: To pay for the research conducted by the University of Newcastle.
options.D: To force companies to remove all accounts belonging to users under 18.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，政府認為提高罰款將迫使社群媒體公司更嚴肅地看待法律，進而加強保護年輕人。選項 A 文章未提及；選項 C 錯誤，罰款與研究經費無關；選項 D 錯誤，法律針對的是 16 歲以下，而非 18 歲。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the effectiveness of current age verification methods used by social media platforms?

options.A: They are highly effective because 5 million accounts have already been removed.
options.B: They are mostly based on advanced biometric technology that is hard to bypass.
options.C: They are currently insufficient because they rely on easily falsifiable information.
options.D: They are perfectly aligned with the requirements set by the eSafety commissioner.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，研究顯示超過 80% 的青少年仍在使用社群媒體，且大多數平台僅要求用戶聲明年齡或上傳普通照片，這些都很容易偽造，顯示目前的驗證機制存在漏洞。選項 A 忽略了政府承認這些數據並不能代表全貌；選項 B 與文中提到的「容易偽造」矛盾；選項 D 文章並未暗示目前機制已達標。

quiz.question: In the third paragraph, what is the meaning of the word 'bypass' as it is used in the context of age restrictions?

options.A: To ignore or find a way around a rule or obstacle.
options.B: To create a new account using a different email address.
options.C: To report a problem to the authorities.
options.D: To improve the security features of a software application.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到青少年正在尋找方法「bypass」年齡限制，隨後解釋為「get around」（繞過或規避）。這表示他們在尋找方法來避開這些規則。選項 B 太過具體；選項 C 與文意相反；選項 D 是指技術升級，而非青少年規避規則的行為。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding Australia's new social media policies?

options.A: The Australian government has successfully eliminated all risks for children on social media.
options.B: Social media companies have voluntarily agreed to cooperate with the new government regulations.
options.C: Other countries are waiting for Australia to fail before they pass their own laws.
options.D: Australia is leading an ambitious but challenging effort to regulate social media for child safety.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章整體討論了澳洲政府如何透過提高罰款與擴大監管權力來保護兒童，同時也誠實地探討了技術上的挑戰與執行成效的疑慮，展現了其作為全球先驅的野心與困境。選項 A 過於樂觀；選項 B 文章未提及公司自願配合；選項 C 錯誤，其他國家是在觀察並準備跟進，而非等待失敗。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
